message:
feat(web): add OperatorSelector component for session prompt builder

Introduces customizable operator assignment for Brenner Protocol sessions.
This enables users to configure which operators are assigned to each role
(Hypothesis Generator, Test Designer, Adversarial Critic) when building
role-specific prompts.

Changes:
- New OperatorSelector.tsx component with:
  - Expandable UI showing all 15 Brenner operators
  - Per-role selection with defaults matching AGENT_ROLES config
  - Visual indicators for selected operators and customizations
  - Reset-to-defaults functionality
  - Touch-optimized interactions with active:scale-95
  - Gradient-styled role cards matching session page aesthetics

- SessionForm.tsx integration:
  - Import OperatorSelector and related types
  - Add operatorSelection state with DEFAULT_OPERATORS initial value
  - (UI wiring to come in follow-up)

- index.ts re-exports:
  - OperatorSelector component
  - DEFAULT_OPERATORS constant
  - OperatorSelection type

Default operators per role:
- Hypothesis Generator: Level-Split, Cross-Domain, Paradox-Hunt
- Test Designer: Exclusion-Test, Materialize, Object-Transpose, Potency-Check
- Adversarial Critic: Exception-Quarantine, Theory-Kill, Scale-Check

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.3.5","title":"Persistence: store artifacts + publish compiled updates","description":"# Goal
Persist session results durably with minimal file sprawl.

Decide:
- repo layout for sessions
- whether compilation writes to repo automatically or via explicit operator command
- how compiled artifacts are posted back to Agent Mail
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:24:23.432232828-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T18:24:23.432232828-05:00","labels":["orchestration","persistence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.3.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:24:23.433091504-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.3.5.1","title":"Decide minimal session artifact storage layout","description":"# Goal
Decide where session artifacts live in the repo (minimize file sprawl) *and* make the location derivable from the thread id.

This must align with:
- thread id conventions (brenner_bot-5so.1.4.1)
- cockpit mapping (brenner_bot-5so.9.1.1)

---

# Design constraints
- Explicit writes only (no surprise persistence).
- Minimal sprawl (prefer 1–2 files per session).
- Deterministic paths derived from `thread_id`.

---

# Candidate layout
- `sessions/<thread_id>.md` (single-file)
- or `sessions/<thread_id>/artifact.md` (+ optional `sessions/index.md`)

---

# Acceptance
- Layout chosen and documented.
- Given a thread id, an operator can predict the path.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.3.5.2","title":"Implement explicit command to write artifact to repo","description":"Implement an explicit operator-triggered write step (avoid surprise writes):
- write compiled artifact to chosen location
- update any index

Done when: artifacts can become durable repo objects.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.4","title":"EPIC: Web App (brennerbot.org)","description":"# Goal
Ship a web app that feels like a **scientific IDE**:
- corpus browse + search + excerpt picker
- session UI (kickoff → responses → compiled artifacts)
- safe public mode + gated lab mode

# Why this matters
The web app is the "front door" for humans; it must make Brenner moves easy to execute and hard to fake.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.4.1","title":"Web: corpus reader UX (markdown render + TOC + anchors)","description":"# Goal
Make reading the corpus pleasant and *citable*:
- render markdown nicely (not raw `<pre>`)
- table of contents
- stable anchor links (esp. `§n`)
- copy citation/excerpt utilities

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.4.1.1","title":"Render corpus docs as markdown (typography, code, links)","description":"Replace raw `<pre>` rendering with a safe markdown renderer (GFM) used for:
- transcript, metaprompts, distillations
- headings/lists/tables/blockquotes
- code blocks with syntax highlighting
- safe link handling
